figures.caption: Figure 1. Schematic of the 5-point-stencil
Table 1. Parameter setting for third and fourth order derivatives

Figure 2. Illustrations of the fitting curves of a) the SLBM and b) the present RSLBM (with parameter choice of ݐଷ = − ଵ  , ݐସ = ఛ ଵଶ − ଵ ଶସ ). The with circles represent the numerical results, the blue dot lines represent the analytical solution and the red solid line represents the fitting curved. All fitting lines correspond to a ܴଶ larger than 0.999.
Figure 3. Numerical dissipation with a zero steady background flow.

Figure 4. Illustrations of the fitting curves of a) the SLBM and b) the present RSLBM (with parameter choice of ݐଷ = − ଵ  , ݐସ = ఛ ଵଶ − ଵ ଶସ ) at ܷ = 0.05. The with circles represent the numerical results, the blue dot lines represent the analytical solution, the red solid lines represent the fitting curves and the green lines represent the envelopes. All fitting lines correspond to a ܴଶ larger than 0.999.
Figure 5. Numerical dispersion and dissipation with a constant background convective flow of ܷ = 0.05 (left) and ܷ = 0.10 (right).

Figure 6. Comparison of relative errors between different methods at Re=10 with ݐ∗ = 0.5 (left) and b) ݐ∗ = 1.0 (right). Slopes of the fitting lines for [LBM, SLBM, MAME-2nd, MAME-4th, present case C, present case E] are [1.9994, 1.9942, 1.9912, 2.0086, 1.9969, 2.0024] (left) and [1.9980, 1.9885, 1.9828, 2.0056, 1.9940, 2.0312] (right).
Table 2. Parameter setting for third and fourth order derivatives with specific values. Here, the relaxation time ߬ = 0.503072 for mesh size of 257 × 257.
Figure 7. The kinetic energy decay of different methods with a mesh size of 257×257 at Re=10000. The reference data is obtained with pseudo-spectral method [45].

Figure 8. The contour plots of vorticity at ݐ∗ = 1.0 from −70 to 70 by an increment of 10. The a) LBM result is simulated at a mesh size of 1025 × 1025 as a reference result while the results, of b) Original SLBM, c) Present case C and d) Present case D, are simulated at a mesh size of 257 × 257.

Figure 9. Distribution of vorticity along the vertical centerline x/L=0.5 for the SLBM and the present RSLBM (case C, case D) at ݐ∗ = 1.0. a) Global view of the curve; b) and c) zoom-in view. The LBM result is simulated at a mesh size of 1025 × 1025 as a reference result.

Figure 10. The contour plots of vorticity at ݐ∗ = 1.0 from −70 to 70 by an increment of 10. The a) LBM results are simulated on the mesh size of 1025 × 1025 as the reference while the results, of b) Present case A, c) Present case B and d) Present case E, are simulated on the mesh size of 257 × 257.

Figure 11. Distribution of vorticity along the vertical centerline x/L=0.5 for the present RSLBM (case A, case B, case E) at ݐ∗ = 1.0. a) Global view of the curve; b) and c) zoom-in view. The LBM results are simulated on the mesh size of 1025 × 1025 as the reference.

Figure 12. The contour plots of vorticity at ݐ∗ = 0.6 and ݐ∗ = 1.0 from −70 to 70 by an increment of 10. a) MAME with second order finite difference; b) MAME with fourth order finite difference; c) Comparison between case C and MAME-4th.
Table 3. Stability examination for different methods at various mesh size. “Y” and “N” denotes the existence of spurious vortex; “…” denotes numerical divergence.

Figure 13. The contour plots of vorticity at ݐ∗ = 1.0 from −70 to 70 by an increment of 10, with underresolved mesh size of 129 × 129. The results are simulated by a) the LBM and b) the present RSLBM (case E).
Figure 14. Configuration of the lid-driven cavity flow.
Figure 15. Illustration of the boundary treatment.
Figure 16. Velocity distributions along centerlines of the lid-driven cavity flow at Re = 7500.
Figure 17. Snapshots of streamlines for the lid-driven cavity flow at Re = 7500 obtained by the present RSLBM and the original SLBM with different mesh spacing.
Table 4. Location of vortices at Re = 7500. X and Y denote the dimensionless coordinates at x and y-directions. “…” denote vortex can’t be resolved.
